observations: Ground-mounted commercial or rural solar array visible — multiple rows of panels on fixed-tilt racking across an open paddock.
Job type is customer pitch; worker is in a sales conversation with a customer.
No customer or worker is visible in the frame — array appears to be a reference site or nearby install used as a demonstration.

salesPitchFeedback.message: Weak opener: 'Uh hi, yeah we're doing solar in the area' is vague and unprofessional. Lead with your name, your company, and a specific local credibility hook — e.g. 'G'day, I'm Jake from SunPower Installs. We've just finished a system two doors down at number 14 and I wanted to pop over while we're in the street.'

No numbers used: 'save some money I reckon' is unconvincing. Customers need a concrete anchor — e.g. 'Most households in this area are saving between $1,800 and $2,400 a year on their power bill. That's roughly a 5 to 6 year payback on a quality system.'

Weak close: 'Do you want to maybe think about it?' signals low confidence and invites a no. Replace with a soft assumptive close — e.g. 'I can get a free proposal put together for your roof this week — are mornings or afternoons better for a quick follow-up call?'

No mention of warranty, product quality, or installer credentials. Customers have heard dodgy solar stories — address it early: 'All our panels carry a 25-year performance warranty and our installs are CEC-accredited, so you're fully covered.'

No urgency or social proof used. Reference the local array or nearby jobs as proof: 'We've got a 50kW ground array just down the road — happy to show you the before-and-after power bills if that helps you decide.'

timeOnTaskNote: Pitch appears to have stalled early — the close was offered too quickly without building enough value. A solid residential door-knock pitch should spend 2 to 3 minutes on rapport and savings before moving to close.
nextSteps: Re-engage the customer with a specific savings figure relevant to their bill size — ask 'What are you paying quarterly at the moment?' to anchor the conversation.
Use the nearby ground-mount array as a visible proof point — point it out or show a photo to build credibility on the spot.
Replace the weak close with a two-option assumptive question about follow-up timing to keep the conversation moving forward.

visualCallouts.label: Upsell proof point
visualCallouts.message: Point to this array as a live example — tell the customer this is the scale of savings you are offering them.

spokenCue.say: Lead with a dollar figure — ditch 'reckon', use real numbers.
